[Comment 6] JIRAUSER62328 (Thu, 29 Jan 2026 17:08:08 +0100):

Security audit of dependencies revealed several dependencies having vulnerabilities. In scope of mitigating them was done:

  • lodash-es 4.17.22 -> 4.17.23
  • @pact-foundation/* - removed completely (see "pact tests" block below)
  • @compodoc/compodoc 1.1.11 -> 1.2.1
  • @stoplight/spectral-cli 6.11.0 -> 6.15.0
  • chai-json - removed completely (no usages in project)
  • @angular-eslint/* - updated to 19.8.1 to match angular version
  • @typescript-eslint/* - updated to latest 8.54.0 to satisfy other dependencies
  • eslint-plugin-jsdoc - 41.1.2 -> 62.3.1
  • webpack-dev-server 4.11.1 -> 5.2.3
  • angular-cli > pacote Angular team not going to fix vulnerability in v19 as it will break compatibility with node 19, since we already using node 22 we can use newer version of pacote which doesn't have vulnerability. => overriden pacote version in package.json

pact tests

Were disabled in 2023 in scope of https://d3.ce.capgemini.com/jira/browse/BNUVZ-5702, and not used since then.
Agreed with Aymen Mastouri that outdated dependencies needed for Pact tests could be removed, and all specs referencing them - excluded from builds (files remains in repo for now).

eslint

Update of eslint brought new linter rules and current codebase is non-compliant to some of them. Those rules were disabled for now, separate task was created for reenabling them and refactor code accordingly

[Comment 9] JIRAUSER62328 (Tue, 27 Jan 2026 15:26:00 +0100):

SASS compiler

Update to angular 19, brought sass@1.85.0 which marks usages of "@import" in scss files as deprecated. As we were using it extensively build- and browser consoles are spammed with warnings.

Was able to migrate to new syntax in stylesheets of components, however not in global stylesheets, which are importing 3rd party stylesheets we have no control over (boostrap4, bnotk/ds-ng).

According to [documentation](https://sass-lang.com/documentation/breaking-changes/import/) there is no plans to remove support of old syntax in nearest future, so we can just *silent* those warnings for now (checked with DS team - this approach was taken). But currently used builder(build-angular:browser) is not supporting configuration for sass. 

Builders

path a) keep webpack with @angular-devkit/build-angular:browser builder

pros:

  • less configuration changes needed, potentially less issues
    cons:
  • to silence sass builder warnings - need to provide custom-webpack config, as build-angular:browser doen't support configuration of sass loader
  • missing percs of modern builders (build speed, bundle size)
  • potential deprecation pressure as angular defaults to new builder and promotes its usage

path b) switch to @angular-devkit/build-angular:application

pros:

  • modern toolset - quicker builds, better dev experience
  • supports configuring sass builder out of the box

cons:

    On leads sync agreed that I'll prepare document describing options with new/old builder, potential consequences, pros/cons and that will be brought to customer to make an informed decision whether we invest time to migration or not.

    [Comment 15] fimisior (Tue, 13 Jan 2026 11:27:55 +0100):

    To upgrade Angular to version 21.x we have to also upgrade environment dependencies: Node.js, TypeScript.
     
    Current state on UVZ:
    node.js v20.18.0
    "typescript": "~5.4.5",
     
    Should be on UVZ:
    node.js v22.12.0
    "typescript": "5.9.0", (5.8.0 with Angular 19.2)
     
    From Angular documentation  RxJS should be set to ^6.5.3 or ^7.4.0, so we are fine with that because we are now using rxjs 7.4.0 version.
     
    This part is basing on:
    https://angular.dev/reference/versions
     
    We should remember to upgrade Angular step by step and verify if app works after each step (manual test on browser, also XNP smoke tests, unit tests and e2e tests should pass)
     
    Step 0: update node.js to  ^22.12.0, typescript to 5.8.0  

     Check if scripts are working fine locally and on Jenkins
    Step 1: Angular 18 to 19
    https://angular.dev/update-guide?v=18.0-19.0&l=3
    Step 2: Angular 19 to 20
    https://angular.dev/update-guide?v=19.0-20.0&l=3 
    Step 3: Angular 20 to 21
    Here we need to upgrade typescript to >=5.9.0 <6.0.0
    https://angular.dev/update-guide?v=20.0-21.0&l=3 
     
    After each step ensure that unit-test are passing and XNP works.
     
    Another topic is that UVZ has DS dependency on master.
    Design System (Pattern Library) relies on: Bootstrap, NG Bootstrap, AG Grid, NG Select, NG Select Highligh.
     
    Dependencies fo

implementation_steps: 1. Upgrade Node.js to version 22 LTS and update TypeScript to the version compatible with Angular 19 (in .nvmrc and CI configuration).
2. Modify package.json to set "@angular/core", "@angular/cli", and all Angular packages to version 19.x, and update Pattern Library to 12.6.0.
3. Run "ng update @angular/core@19 @angular/cli@19" to apply Angular migrations.
4. Update third‑party libraries: ng-bootstrap (apply patch from UVZUSLNVV-5824), ng-select, ng-option-highlight, ag-grid-angular, ag-grid-community to versions compatible with Angular 19.
5. Adjust imports and API usages in each affected component (e.g., ActionRestServiceImpl, KeyManagerRestServiceImpl, ResourceFactory, etc.) to resolve deprecation warnings introduced by Angular 19.
6. Verify that the vertical action bar component continues to compile and render; add compatibility shims if required (still supported until PL 13.2.0).
7. Remove code paths, modules, and styles that belong exclusively to Angular 18 or Pattern Library 11.3.1 and are not needed by Angular 19 / PL 12.6.0.
8. Update linting and build scripts to use the new @angular-eslint version (19.8.1) and @typescript-eslint 8.54.0.
9. Run existing unit tests, fix failures, and ensure ResourceFactoryTest passes with the new framework version.
10. Execute integration tests for all REST interfaces listed, confirming that controllers (e.g., ActionRestServiceImpl, KeyManagerRestServiceImpl) behave correctly after the upgrade.
11. Perform a security audit of updated dependencies (lodash-es, @compodoc, @stoplight/spectral-cli, webpack-dev-server, pacote override) and address any newly reported vulnerabilities.
12. Deploy the upgraded application to a staging environment, run smoke tests (including re‑encryption tests once Cloudbeaver is functional), and validate end‑to‑end flows.

unit_tests: Run all existing unit tests and update failing tests to match Angular 19 APIs (e.g., ComponentFixture usage).
Add unit tests for any newly introduced compatibility shims for the vertical action bar.
integration_tests: Execute integration tests for each REST endpoint to verify request/response contracts after controller modifications.
Perform end‑to‑end UI tests covering critical user journeys that involve ng-bootstrap and ag‑grid components.

security_considerations: Ensure CORS configuration allows only trusted origins (pattern: cors in ActionRestService).
Verify CSRF protection is enabled for all state‑changing operations (pattern: csrf in AbstractSecurityConfiguration).
Apply pre_authorize checks on KeyManagerRestService methods (pattern: pre_authorize in KeyManagerRestService).
validation_strategy: Add @NotNull annotations to DeedEntry fields to enforce non‑null values (pattern: not_null on DeedEntry).
Add @Valid annotations to DeedEntryRestService method parameters (pattern: valid on DeedEntryRestService).
Add @NotBlank annotations to KmConfig fields (pattern: not_blank on KmConfig).
Add @Min and @Max constraints to numeric KmConfig properties (patterns: min on KmConfig, max on KmConfig).
error_handling: Create or reuse custom RuntimeException classes for domain‑specific errors (pattern: RuntimeException custom_exception).
Add a global @ExceptionHandler for generic Exception to return standardized error responses (pattern: Exception exception_handler).
Add @ExceptionHandler for HttpClientErrorException to map client errors to proper HTTP status codes (pattern: HttpClientErrorException exception_handler).
Add @ExceptionHandler for HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException to return 405 responses (pattern: HttpRequestMethodNotSupportedException exception_handler).

layer_compliance: Check if changes follow layered architecture

development_plan.complexity_reasoning: The upgrade spans a major Angular version and requires coordinated updates of multiple third‑party libraries, code refactoring for deprecations, and removal of legacy artifacts, but the codebase is modular and the existing test suite provides good safety nets.
risks: Breaking changes in Angular 19 may cause runtime errors in legacy components.
Incompatible versions of third‑party libraries (ng-bootstrap, ag‑grid) could delay the upgrade.
Vertical action bar deprecation may lead to UI regressions if not properly shimmed.
Security audit may uncover new vulnerabilities in transitive dependencies.
